title: Measure folded reducer with registered producer coupling
motivation: The standalone folded reducer boundary sweep exposed producer-side partial fragments as top-level macro IO and ppc4 failed under pin pressure. The internal-source sweep removed those pins and showed ppc4 can be physically implemented, but its source was only a combinational counter stub. The next missing measurement is a producer-coupled proxy with registered internal handoff.
hypothesis: A registered producer-to-reducer handoff should remain more feasible than the standalone boundary wrapper while adding realistic producer register, fanout, and ready/valid control cost relative to the pure internal-source proxy.

expected_behavior.functional: The folded reducer RTL remains exact and unchanged; the wrapper only supplies deterministic producer fragments through internal registered nets.
expected_behavior.timing_model: Reducer latency remains partials / partials_per_cycle accepted chunks, with one producer register stage feeding the reducer.
expected_behavior.physical_model: Producer-side fragment buses are internal registered nets, consumer-side reduced outputs and counters remain top-level macro pins, and ppc controls internal fanout plus reducer arithmetic width.

risks: The deterministic producer is still a proxy; it does not include the full attention-tile datapath or NoC scheduler.
The extra producer registers make area and power larger than the internal-source proxy, but this is intentional for a more realistic local-cluster estimate.
If ppc4 fails again, the result must separate internal routing/fanout pressure from top-level pin-site pressure observed in the standalone boundary sweep.
